commonErrors.message: Project type did not match expectation

summary: The client wants to build a food delivery application.
The application should include features for users, restaurants, delivery tracking, payments, ratings, and an admin dashboard.
explicit_requirements: Restaurant onboarding (registration, menu management, order handling)
Live delivery tracking (real-time updates for users and potentially restaurants/admins)
Ratings and reviews system (for users to rate restaurants/dishes, and potentially for drivers)
Admin dashboard (for managing users, restaurants, orders, content, etc.)
assumptions: The client has a clear vision for the user interface (UI) and user experience (UX).
The client has defined or will define the target platforms (iOS, Android, Web).
The client understands the need for backend infrastructure to support the application.
The client has a budget allocated for the development, testing, and deployment of the application.
The client will provide necessary branding assets (logos, color schemes).
missing_information: Specific details on user roles and permissions (e.g., customer, restaurant owner, delivery driver, admin).
Payment gateway preferences or existing relationships.
Detailed requirements for the admin dashboard functionality.
Scope and features for the live delivery tracking (e.g., GPS integration, driver app requirements).
Technology stack preferences (if any).
Target launch date and any phased rollout plans.
Definition of 'restaurant onboarding' - what specific fields and information are needed?
Any specific analytics or reporting requirements.

modules.description: Handles all aspects of user accounts, authentication, and profile management for customers.
Enables restaurants to manage their profiles, menus, and incoming orders.
Manages the lifecycle of an order from placement to completion.
Provides real-time tracking of delivery personnel and order status for users, restaurants, and admins.
features: Live location updates for delivery drivers
Real-time order status tracking for users

modules.description: Handles secure processing of payments for orders.
Allows users to rate and review restaurants, dishes, and potentially delivery drivers.
A backend interface for administrators to manage the entire application ecosystem.
Manages and sends notifications to users, restaurants, and potentially delivery drivers for various events.

summary: The client wants to develop an e-commerce web platform.
The platform should include core e-commerce functionalities such as catalog search, shopping carts, and checkout.
Additional features required are order tracking for customers, seller management capabilities, and an analytics dashboard.
assumptions: The client has a defined target audience for the e-commerce platform.
The client has existing products or a plan to source products for the catalog.
There is a need for user roles (customer, seller, administrator) within the platform.
Payment gateway integration will be required for the checkout process.
Security measures for handling customer and payment data will be necessary.
missing_information: Specific technologies or frameworks preferred for development.
Detailed requirements for the catalog search (e.g., filtering, sorting options).
User interface (UI) and user experience (UX) design preferences.
Scalability requirements for the platform.
Integration needs with existing systems (e.g., CRM, ERP).
Budget and timeline for the project.
Specific metrics to be tracked in the analytics dashboard.
Details on seller management features (e.g., product upload, commission structure, payout management).

modules.description: Handles user registration, login, roles (customer, seller, administrator), and access control.
Manages product information, categories, inventory, and provides search and filtering capabilities.
Allows customers to add, remove, and manage items in their shopping cart before checkout.
Facilitates the checkout process, including shipping information, payment selection, and integration with payment gateways.
Handles order processing, tracking, and history for both customers and administrators.
Provides tools and interfaces for sellers to manage their products, orders, and performance.
Collects and presents data on sales, customer behavior, and platform performance.
Underlying services for platform deployment, scaling, and data security.

errors: Project requirements are too short. Please provide more details.

summary: The client has provided a very unstructured and unclear requirement.
The client is exploring potential ideas for an application or AI solution, but is unsure of the direction.
The input contains random characters and phrases, making it difficult to interpret a specific need.
assumptions: The client is in the very early stages of brainstorming.
The client is open to suggestions and needs help defining their project.
missing_information: What is the core problem the client wants to solve?
What are the business objectives for a potential app or AI solution?
What are the client's priorities and constraints?
What is the desired outcome or success metric?
Is there a specific industry or domain in mind?
What is the budget and timeline?

summary: The client wants a hospital management application.
Key features include patient records, doctor scheduling, billing, and HIPAA-style security.
assumptions: The client expects the application to be accessible by authorized hospital staff.
The billing system should handle various payment methods and insurance claims.
HIPAA compliance implies robust data encryption, access controls, and audit trails.
missing_information: Specific details on the scope of patient records (e.g., medical history, demographics, contact info).
Requirements for doctor scheduling (e.g., real-time availability, shift management, on-call scheduling).
Detailed billing requirements (e.g., insurance integration, payment gateway, reporting needs).
Specific security protocols and certifications required for HIPAA compliance.
User roles and permissions (e.g., administrator, doctor, nurse, billing staff, patient portal).
Integration needs with existing hospital systems.
Platform preference (web, mobile - iOS, Android, or both).
Timeline and budget constraints.

summary: The client wants to build a CRM platform for their sales team.
assumptions: The platform will be used by a sales team.
The client expects standard CRM functionalities beyond what's explicitly listed.
missing_information: Specific details about lead management features (e.g., lead scoring, lead assignment).
Details on follow-up mechanisms (e.g., automated reminders, email integration).
Types of dashboards and key metrics required.
Specific roles and permissions to be configured.
Integration requirements with other systems.
Technology stack preferences.
Deployment environment (cloud, on-premise).
Budget and timeline.

summary: Develop an enterprise operations platform for a logistics company.
The platform should encompass a wide range of functionalities including shipment planning, route optimization, warehouse operations, vendor management, and billing.
It needs to include analytics dashboards, mobile access for field staff, audit logging, and document management.
Key technical requirements include Single Sign-On (SSO), configurable permissions, API integrations, and Service Level Agreement (SLA) monitoring with alerting.
Reporting capabilities should include region-wise data.
The system must also address non-functional requirements such as high concurrency, disaster recovery, data retention policies, and support for a phased rollout strategy.
assumptions: The client has a defined logistics business process that needs to be digitized and optimized.
Existing systems may need integration, hence the requirement for API integrations.
There is a need for secure access and user role management, indicated by SSO and configurable permissions.
Real-time or near real-time data is crucial for operations and analytics.
The company operates in multiple regions, requiring specific reporting capabilities.
The platform is intended for internal use and potentially external partners or field agents.
Compliance with data retention policies is a business or legal requirement.
missing_information: Specific details on the scale of operations (e.g., number of users, daily shipments, warehouse size).
Current technology stack or existing infrastructure for integration assessment.
Detailed workflows for each functional area (shipment planning, warehouse ops, billing, etc.).
Specific performance metrics for high concurrency and SLA monitoring.
Disaster recovery objectives (RTO/RPO - Recovery Time Objective/Recovery Point Objective).
Data retention policy specifics (e.g., how long data must be retained, archival strategy).
Security standards and compliance requirements beyond SSO.
User interface/user experience (UI/UX) expectations for different user roles.
Budget and timeline constraints.
Details on the 'vendor management' module (e.g., types of vendors, tracking needs).
Specific analytics to be displayed on dashboards.
Nature of API integrations required (inbound/outbound, specific systems).

roles.title: Lead Backend Engineer
roles.description: Responsible for server-side logic, API development, database management, and authentication implementation.

roles.title: Frontend Engineer
roles.description: Develops the user interface, ensures a responsive and interactive dashboard experience, and integrates with backend APIs.

roles.title: DevOps Engineer
roles.description: Manages infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, deployment, monitoring, and scaling of the application.

roles.title: UI/UX Designer
roles.description: Designs the user experience and interface for the dashboard, focusing on usability, clarity, and data visualization.

roles.title: Product Manager
roles.description: Defines product vision, prioritizes features, gathers user feedback, and ensures the product meets market needs.

team.size_recommendation: Start with a lean team of 3-5 engineers plus a designer and product manager. Roles can be combined in the early stages (e.g., a full-stack engineer).
team.scaling_plan: As the product grows, consider adding specialized roles like dedicated QA engineers, data scientists, and more frontend/backend developers.

backend.authentication: JWT (JSON Web Tokens) for stateless authentication, OAuth for third-party integrations

frontend.ui_library: Material-UI (React) or Vuetify (Vue.js) for pre-built components
frontend.data_visualization: Chart.js, D3.js, or Recharts for interactive charts and graphs

devops.cloud_provider: AWS, Google Cloud Platform, or Azure
devops.orchestration: Kubernetes (optional for early stages, consider serverless/PaaS)
devops.ci_cd: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins
devops.monitoring: Prometheus & Grafana, or cloud-native monitoring services (CloudWatch, Stackdriver)

reporting.generation: Backend service with PDF/CSV generation libraries
reporting.delivery: Email services (SendGrid, AWS SES) or in-app download options
reporting.scheduling: Cron jobs or background task queues (Celery, BullMQ)

summary: Develop a comprehensive food delivery application.
The app should cater to users ordering food, restaurants managing their presence, and administrators overseeing operations.
explicit_requirements: User Account Management: Users should be able to create and manage their accounts.
Restaurant Onboarding: A system for restaurants to register and manage their profiles.
Live Delivery Tracking: Real-time tracking of food delivery progress.
Payment Gateway Integration: Secure payment processing for orders.
Ratings and Reviews: Functionality for users to rate and review restaurants and dishes.
Admin Dashboard: An interface for administrators to manage the platform, users, restaurants, orders, etc.
assumptions: The platform will support multiple restaurants.
There will be a need for delivery personnel management (though not explicitly stated, it's implied by 'live delivery tracking').
Standard e-commerce workflows for ordering and payment will be followed.
The app will be available on major mobile platforms (iOS/Android) and potentially a web interface for restaurants/admins.
missing_information: Specific technology stack preferences (e.g., native, hybrid, web frameworks).
Scalability requirements and expected user/restaurant volume.
Detailed features for the admin dashboard (e.g., analytics, reporting, dispute resolution).
Specific payment gateway requirements or preferred providers.
User interface (UI) and user experience (UX) design guidelines or preferences.
Integration points with third-party services (e.g., mapping services, SMS notifications).
Budget and timeline constraints.
Details on delivery personnel management (registration, assignment, tracking capabilities).
Support for different user roles within restaurants (e.g., owner, manager, staff).
Geographic region of operation.

modules.description: Handles all aspects of user accounts, including registration, authentication, profile management, and authorization.
features: Role-based access control (user, restaurant staff, admin)

modules.description: Enables restaurants to manage their presence on the platform, including profile information, menus, and operational settings.
features: Restaurant profile management (name, description, cuisine, hours)
Support for multiple restaurant staff roles

modules.description: Manages the entire lifecycle of a customer's order, from placement to fulfillment.
Handles secure processing of payments for customer orders.
features: Integration with selected payment gateways (e.g., Stripe, PayPal)

modules.description: Facilitates the management and tracking of delivery personnel and their assignments.
features: Delivery personnel registration and profile management
Real-time location tracking of delivery personnel
Delivery status updates (picked up, en route, delivered)
Integration with mapping services for route optimization and tracking

modules.description: Allows users to provide feedback on restaurants and dishes, and view existing reviews.
features: User ability to rate restaurants and dishes
Display of ratings and reviews for restaurants and dishes

modules.description: Provides administrators with tools to oversee and manage the entire food delivery platform.
features: Restaurant management (approve, reject, manage restaurant details)

modules.description: Manages the sending of timely notifications to users, restaurants, and delivery personnel.
features: Notifications for order status, delivery updates, promotions, etc.

summary: Develop an e-commerce web platform.
assumptions: The platform will support multiple sellers.
Users will be able to browse products, add them to a cart, and complete a purchase.
Sellers will have tools to manage their products and view orders.
Data for analytics will be collected and displayed.
missing_information: Specific technologies or tech stack preferences.
Payment gateway integrations required.
Shipping and tax calculation logic.
User roles and permissions (customer, seller, admin).
Details on the analytics metrics and reporting needs.
Scalability requirements.
Design and UI/UX considerations.

modules.description: Manages product information, categories, and search functionality.
Handles user registration, authentication, profiles, and roles.
Manages the user's shopping cart before checkout.
Facilitates the entire checkout flow from cart to order placement.
Handles order processing, tracking, and history for both customers and sellers.
Provides tools for sellers to manage their products, orders, and storefront.
Integrates with external payment providers to process transactions.
Collects and visualizes key business metrics for administrators and sellers.
Handles sending out various notifications to users and sellers.

errors: Project requirements are too short. Please provide more details.

summary: Client expressed random ideas, including the possibility of an app or an AI project, but is also uncertain about pursuing anything.
The input contains significant noise and unclear intent.
assumptions: The client is in the early stages of ideation.
The client's current input is highly speculative and not a firm commitment.
missing_information: Clarification on any specific business problem or opportunity.
Definition of project goals or desired outcomes.
Preference between app development or AI implementation.
Budget or timeline considerations.
Any constraints or key stakeholders.

summary: Create a hospital management application.
The application should include modules for patient records, doctor scheduling, and billing.
Implement HIPAA-level security.
assumptions: The client wants a new application, not an upgrade to an existing one.
HIPAA compliance is a critical requirement for the application's security.
The target audience includes patients, doctors, and administrative staff.
missing_information: Specific details on the patient record fields required.
Detailed workflow for doctor scheduling (e.g., appointment booking, cancellations, reschedules).
Payment methods to be supported in the billing module.
Specific HIPAA security measures to be implemented (e.g., encryption standards, access controls, audit trails).
Integration requirements with any existing hospital systems.
User roles and permissions within the application.
Platform requirements (web, mobile - iOS/Android, or both).

summary: Build a CRM platform for the sales team.
assumptions: The client has a defined sales process that the CRM should support.
The sales team requires specific reporting and analytics capabilities.
User roles and their corresponding permissions need to be defined.
missing_information: Specific details about the sales process.
Required dashboard metrics and KPIs.
Definition of user roles and their permissions.
Integration requirements with other systems (e.g., email, calendar, marketing automation).
Data migration strategy from existing systems (if any).
Scalability and performance expectations.
Technology stack preferences or constraints.

summary: Develop an enterprise operations platform for a logistics company.
The platform needs to encompass a wide range of functionalities including shipment planning, route optimization, warehouse operations, vendor management, and billing workflows.
It should also feature analytics dashboards and provide mobile access for field operations.

Key technical and operational requirements include audit logs, document uploads, Single Sign-On (SSO), configurable permissions, API integrations, Service Level Agreement (SLA) monitoring, alerting, and region-wise reporting.
Furthermore, the system must be designed for high concurrency, disaster recovery, adherence to data retention policies, and support for a phased rollout.
assumptions: The client has existing logistics operations that require streamlining.
The logistics company has a defined user base for the platform (e.g., operations managers, warehouse staff, drivers, vendors, finance personnel).
The client has existing IT infrastructure and systems with which API integrations might be necessary.
Security and compliance are important considerations for an enterprise logistics platform.
missing_information: Specific details on the scale of operations (e.g., number of shipments, warehouses, users, geographic regions).
Detailed workflows for each functional area (shipment planning, route optimization, warehouse ops, billing, vendor management).
Specific requirements for analytics dashboards (key metrics, visualizations).
Technical specifications for mobile field access (platform - iOS/Android, offline capabilities).
Details on required API integrations (e.g., ERP, TMS, WMS, carrier systems).
Definition of Service Level Agreements (SLAs) to be monitored.
Specific alerting scenarios and notification methods.
Geographic scope for region-wise reporting.
Technical architecture preferences or constraints.
Data retention policy specifics (duration, archival strategy).
Details on the desired phased rollout strategy and timelines.
Budget and timeline expectations.
Existing systems and technology stack.
User roles and their specific permissions.
